turn_details.question: What is the capital of Australia?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:0] The capital of Australia is Canberra

turn_details.question: Which continent is Australia located in?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:1] Australia is a country in Oceania

turn_details.question: What is the largest city in Australia?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:3] Sydney is the largest city in Australia but not the capital

turn_details.question: What language is most widely spoken in Australia?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:4] English is the de facto national language of Australia

turn_details.question: What is the second largest city in Australia?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:7] Melbourne is the second largest city in Australia

turn_details.question: What stars appear on the Australian flag?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:8] The Australian flag features the Union Jack and Southern Cross

turn_details.question: What famous rock formation is in central Australia?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:9] Uluru is a large sandstone monolith in central Australia

turn_details.question: What native marsupial is mentioned for Australia?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:10] Kangaroos are native marsupials found across Australia

turn_details.question: What term describes remote arid interior of Australia?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:11] The Outback refers to remote arid interior regions of Australia

turn_details.question: Why was Canberra chosen as capital?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:12] Canberra was selected as capital as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne

turn_details.question: At what temperature does water boil at standard pressure?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:0] Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure

turn_details.question: At what temperature does water freeze?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:1] Water freezes at 0 degrees Celsius at standard pressure

turn_details.question: What is the chemical formula for water?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:5] The chemical formula for water is H2O

turn_details.question: Does salt water boil at same temperature as pure water?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:6] Salt water boils at a slightly higher temperature than pure water

turn_details.question: What process turns liquid water into vapor?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:7] Evaporation turns liquid water into water vapor

turn_details.question: What process forms clouds from vapor?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:8] Condensation forms clouds when water vapor cools

turn_details.question: Which elements combine to form water?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:9] Hydrogen and oxygen combine to form water

turn_details.question: Does water expand or contract when it freezes?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:10] Water expands when it freezes, unlike most liquids

turn_details.question: What process separates water from dissolved salts?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:11] Distillation separates water from dissolved salts by boiling

turn_details.question: What pH is neutral for pure water?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:12] pH 7 is neutral for pure water at 25C

turn_details.question: What is the SI unit of force?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:0] The SI unit of force is the Newton

turn_details.question: In F=ma, what does m represent?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:4] Mass is measured in kilograms in the SI system

turn_details.question: In F=ma, what does a represent?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:5] Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity

turn_details.question: What is weight in terms of force and gravity?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:6] Weight is force due to gravity on a mass

turn_details.question: What quantity combines speed with direction?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:7] Velocity is speed with direction

turn_details.question: What energy depends on mass and velocity squared?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:8] Kinetic energy is one half m v squared

turn_details.question: What force opposes relative motion between surfaces?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:9] Friction opposes relative motion between surfaces

turn_details.question: What is approximate gravity on Earth in m/s²?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:10] Gravity on Earth is approximately 9

turn_details.answer: [corpus:11] Momentum equals mass times velocity

turn_details.question: What is force times distance in direction of force?
turn_details.answer: [corpus:12] Work is force times distance in the direction of force
